Component is declared as `const ReviewCard = (...)` (line 14) — PascalCase.
Line 3: `export interface ReviewCardProps {` — matches the *Props suffix convention.
Lines 36-49: Avatar section with data-testid="review-card-avatar" renders an when avatarUrl is provided, or initials fallback in a styled div.
Lines 59-79: Star rating section with data-testid="review-card-rating" renders 5 SVG stars, coloring filled stars amber and unfilled stars zinc. Rating prop typed as `1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5`.
Tailwind classes used extensively throughout: flex, gap-3, rounded-xl, border, bg-white, text-sm, font-semibold, dark: variants, h-10, w-10, etc.

suggestions.reason: This is subsumed by the more specific 'not-flat-file' and 'component-index-tsx' assertions. It would pass even for an empty file at any path containing 'ReviewCard'. Consider checking that the file contains a valid default export.

No assertion checks that the component uses an arrow function with default export (a core tcp convention). The executor ran tcp validate-file which checks this, but the eval doesn't verify it independently.
No assertion verifies the showcase page was updated to import from the convention path. The transcript shows this was a key part of the work, but it goes unchecked.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are solid for structural conventions and UI element presence. Main gap is no verification of arrow-function export style or integration (showcase page import path).
